title: Self-Organizing Workers
description: Workers can claim tasks from a board and continue with simple local rules.

steps.content: Let the workers pick up the remaining cleanup tasks on their own.
steps.annotation: The request delegates control to the runtime, not a central planner.
The shared board receives a new claimable task.
The task now exists for any worker to discover.
An idle cycle is where autonomous workers poll inboxes and scan for work.

steps.content: worker-b claimed board-3 and sent a progress update
steps.annotation: A local policy plus shared state is enough to produce autonomous progress.
The lead can still inspect the shared state at any time.
Autonomy remains observable because it is grounded in shared runtime state.

steps.content: A worker picked up the cleanup task without central assignment, and the board reflects the new owner.
steps.annotation: Self-organization emerges from simple runtime rules.
